title: ECI announces election schedule for 4 States, Puducherry; two-phase polling in West Bengal
content: Kerala, Assam and Puducherry will go to the polls on April 9, while Tamil Nadu will vote in a single phase on April 23. Voting in West Bengal will be held in multiple phases beginning April 23

title: Environment Ministry endorses names of 10-member expert committee set up to define Aravalli hills

title: Trinamool MP Mahua Moitra says BJP workers brought ‘jungle raj’ in West Bengal

title: Odisha BJP shifts its MLAs to Paradip ahead of Rajya Sabha polls

title: In frames | War lights a kitchen fire
content: West Asian conflict has snuffed out a clean cooking fuel in kitchens; facing a crisis, homes and eateries are looking at firewood and biomass to energise their cooking

title: Watch: HPV vaccination roll out, climate change, metabolic diseases and more | Health Wrap
content: In this episode of Health Wrap, senior health journalists Ramya Kannan and Zubeda Hamid discuss the week’s important developments in public health. This episode looks at the rollout of Human papillomavirus (HPV) vaccination across States in India to prevent cervical cancer, which continues to claim thousands of lives each year.

title: ECI to announce Assembly poll dates for four States, one Union Territory today
content: The poll authority has convened a press conference at 4 p.m. to announce the schedule for Assam, Kerala, West Bengal, Tamil Nadu and the Union Territory of Puducherry

title: Fire breaks out at Nature Bazaar in south Delhi, around 50 shops gutted
content: According to the DFS, a call regarding the blaze was received at 7.37 a.m., following which 10 fire tenders were rushed to the spot to control the flames. No injury to anyone has been reported so far.

title: India well-placed to deal with any situation on crude oil, fuel amid West Asia supply shock: Piyush Goyal
content: Acknowledging that while the country is facing "stress" on the rupee, Mr. Goyal said there is no reason for anyone to panic as rupee is expected to bounce back in a more gradual fashion, possibly once the war is over

title: Fact check: Are “40 out of 100 children malnourished” in Gujarat?
content: Data indicates that the statement is true and that several of the worst affected districts are tribal ones, pointing to a higher burden of malnutrition among adivasi communities in the State

title: Gateway to the world, from Madurai
content: In the light of the Union government granting international airport status to the Madurai airport, S. Sundar traces the long history of such a demand and highlights what the new status will mean for the ever-growing tourism, connectivity, and industrial needs of districts in southern Tamil Nadu

content: The RedHawks and Tigers continue to be the talk of the bubble, plus more considerations for men's committee.

content: No. 1 Duke defeated No. 10 Virginia for its second straight ACC tournament championship under coach Jon Scheyer and its third title in the past four years.

title: Venezuela KO's defending champ Japan from WBC
content: Wilyer Abreu hit a go-ahead, three-run shot in the sixth as Venezuela rallied to stun defending champion Japan and advance to the World Baseball Classic semifinals.

title: DeChambeau wins playoff as foe misses 2-footer
content: Bryson DeChambeau hit his drive into the water and still won the playoff with a par at LIV Golf Singapore after Richard T. Lee of Canada missed a 2-foot putt.

content: No. 2 seed Cal Baptist beat top-seeded Utah Valley 63-61 to win the final Western Athletic Conference tournament after the Wolverines' Isaac Davis missed a potential tying alley-oop dunk with three seconds left.

title: Reaves, Luka deliver late-game magic in OT thriller
content: Austin Reaves executed a perfect missed free throw before sinking the tying bucket to force overtime, and Luka Doncic drained the winning jumper in the extra frame to lift the Lakers past the Nuggets.

content: Olympic champion Mikaela Shiffrin won her record-equaling eighth World Cup slalom of the season Sunday.

title: Kerala's Pinarayi Vijayan To Contest From Dharmadam, CPM From 86 Seats
content: Kerala Assembly Polls Live Updates 2026: Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan will contest the state assembly election -- due on April 9 -- from Dharmadam. His party will contest on 86 seats. Vijayan contested from the same seat in 2016 and 2021.

title: Iran Warns Of '9/11 Style' False Flag Plot By 'Epstein Network'
content: Ali Larijani, Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council, has warned of what he described as a possible conspiracy aimed at blaming Iran for a major terrorist attack.

title: Kerala Goes To Polls On April 9, Results Out On May 4
content: Kerala, which was recently rechristened as Keralam, will vote in a single phase on April 9. The votes for the 140-member assembly will be counted on May 4.

title: Puducherry Goes To Polls On April 9, Votes To Be Counted On May 4
content: Union Territory of Puducherry will go to polls on April 9 for all 30 constituencies. The votes, along with four other states - Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Bengal and Assam - will be counted on May 4.

title: When Will Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam, Puducherry Vote? See Full Schedule
content: The Election Commission today announced dates for Assembly polls in Bengal, Assam, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, and the Union Territory of Puducherry.

title: Bypolls To 8 Assembly Seats On April 9 And 23, Counting On May 4: Poll Body
content: Bypolls to five assembly seats in Goa, Karnataka, Nagaland and Tripura will be held on April 9 and in another three constituencies of Gujarat and Maharashtra on April 23, Chief Election Commissioner (CEC) Gynaesh Kumar announced on Sunday.

title: Gen Z To Drive $1.3 Trillion Spending In India By 2030: Report
content: By 2030, Gen Z will comprise 27 per cent of India's population and will command USD 1.3 trillion in consumption, reshaping consumer markets with a focus on experiences, sustainability and digital convenience, according to a report.

title: End Of "Ethnic Militancy": Assam Signs Final Pacts With 4 Armed Groups
content: The Assam government on Sunday signed memoranda of settlement with three Kuki armed groups and one Hmar outfit, claiming that the agreements mark the end of "ethnic militancy" in the state, an official statement said.

title: Assembly Poll Dates Out For West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Kerala, Assam And Puducherry

title: Kerala, Assam & Puducherry vote on April 9, TN on April 23, 2-phase polling in WB; results May 4
content: The Election Commission has unveiled the assembly poll schedule for West Bengal, Tamil Nadu, Assam, Kerala, and Puducherry. Key political players like Mamata Banerjee, MK Stalin, and Himanta Biswa Sarma are gearing up for intense electoral battles across these states and the Union Territory.

title: No Hitman! Virat Kohli picks Chris Gayle over Rohit Sharma as best T20 opener
content: Virat Kohli, in a recent 'This or That' challenge, named Chris Gayle as his top T20 opener over Rohit Sharma.  Kohli's selections progressed through Adam Gilchrist, Travis Head, and Virender Sehwag before reaching the final showdown. Gayle's prolific T20 record, including the highest individual IPL score, solidifies his legendary status in the format.

title: Perplexity AI CEO agrees that Computer Science is gradually returning to its roots
content: AI is rapidly automating coding's routine tasks, pushing software engineering back towards its mathematical roots. Experts predict AI could soon handle most end-to-end coding, with some engineers already code-free. While LLMs still struggle with complex design, this shift is prompting a rethink in computer science education, emphasizing logic over syntax.

title: IEA’s record oil release may offer only limited relief if Hormuz stays shut: S&P
content: The International Energy Agency's historic 400 million barrel oil reserve release offers limited relief as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed. S&P Global Energy warns this move won't solve the core issue of disrupted shipping, leaving Asian markets particularly vulnerable.  Markets face continued imbalance until this critical chokepoint reopens.

title: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet to company’s employees: If you want a promotion, you need to ...
content: Accenture CEO Julie Sweet has mandated that employees must demonstrate proficiency in using AI tools to be considered for promotions. This requirement reflects the consultancy's integration of AI into its core operations, a shift Sweet likens to the adoption of computers in the past. The company has invested significantly in reskilling its workforce to adapt to these evolving workplace technologies.

title: PM Modi lauds Om Birla's leadership after no-trust motion fails; LS Speaker reacts
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi lauded Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla for his composure and impartiality following the rejection of a no-confidence motion against him. Modi commended Birla's adherence to parliamentary decorum and his affirmation that no one is above the rules, reinforcing democratic traditions.

title: 'Babar Azam doesn't have a voice': Ex-Pak batter cites Kohli example in T20 WC rant
content: Former Pakistan batter Basit Ali criticized the decision to bat Babar Azam at No.4 during the T20 World Cup, suggesting he should have opposed the move like Virat Kohli. However, Kamran Akmal disagreed, stating players rarely challenge management, especially when out of form.

title: US-Iran war: Trump’s AI czar says that this is a good time to declare victory and get out
content: White House AI and crypto czar David Sacks warned of catastrophic consequences if the US-Israel conflict with Iran continues. He highlighted the potential for devastating attacks on oil and gas infrastructure, rendering the Gulf uninhabitable due to water scarcity. Sacks also cautioned that prolonged conflict could lead to Israel's destruction.

title: EC announces bypoll dates for 8 assembly constituencies across six states; check schedule
content: By-elections for eight assembly seats across six states, including Goa, Gujarat, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Nagaland, and Tripura, have been announced by the Election Commission. Polling dates are set for April 9 and April 23, with vote counting scheduled for May 4. These elections will fill vacant legislative seats, ensuring full representation in state assemblies.

title: Mamata raises monthly honorarium for priests, muezzins ahead of poll schedule announcement
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee announced a Rs 500 hike in monthly honorariums for priests and muezzins, raising their pay to Rs 2,000. This move, along with the approval of all new applications, was made just before the Election Commission declared poll dates. Banerjee stated the decision recognizes the vital role of religious functionaries in community life.

title: Mother quit job, son scripts history: How Mayank, 16, became Northeast’s 1st GM
content: Sixteen-year-old Mayank Chakraborty has made history as the first Grandmaster from Assam and Northeast India. His landmark achievement in Sweden follows years of immense sacrifice, including his mother, Dr. Monomita Chakraborty, resigning from her medical career to support his journey. While celebrated as India’s 94th GM, Mayank’s family now seeks government and corporate backing to sustain his elite professional career.

title: Punjab man shot dead in Canada: How passenger pickup from motel ended in fatal shooting
content: A 30-year-old Punjabi cab driver, Devinder Singh, was tragically shot and killed in North Battleford, Canada, while on a pickup. Police are investigating the homicide, which occurred when a man reportedly followed a female passenger and opened fire. Devinder, who had moved to Canada four years ago, was a driver for Crown Cab and co-owned a café with his wife.

title: West Bengal elections: Voting in two phases from April 23 - check full schedule
content: The Trinamool Congress won a decisive victory in the 2021 West Bengal Assembly elections, securing a third consecutive term. The party claimed 213 seats in the 294-member assembly. The Bharatiya Janata Party emerged as the principal opposition, winning 77 seats. This election marked a significant shift in the state's political landscape.

title: 2017 Champions Trophy-winning captain Sarfaraz Ahmed retires from all forms of cricket
content: Former Pakistan captain Sarfaraz Ahmed has announced his retirement from all formats of cricket. The wicketkeeper-batter, who led Pakistan to major ICC tournament final victories, will now focus on administrative and mentoring roles. His retirement opens the door for a potential long-term appointment as the national Test team's head coach.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: Voting in one phase on April 23, results on May 4
content: Tamil Nadu gears up for assembly elections on April 23, with vote counting on May 4. The DMK-led alliance seeks to retain power against the AIADMK-NDA bloc. Notably, actor Vijay's new party, Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam, has emerged as a significant contender, aiming to disrupt the established political landscape.

title: Puducherry assembly polls: Key election dates and other details
content: Puducherry will vote for its 30 Assembly seats on April 9, 2026, with results announced on May 4. Nearly 9.44 lakh voters are eligible, including 23,033 first-time electors. The Election Commission emphasized free and fair polls, deploying extensive webcasting and model polling stations. The NDA, including AINRC and BJP, has finalized its seat-sharing plan.

title: Rahul Gandhi demands Bharat Ratna for BSP founder Kanshi Ram; writes to PM Modi
content: Rahul Gandhi has urged Prime Minister Narendra Modi to posthumously award the Bharat Ratna to Bahujan Samaj Party founder Kanshi Ram. Gandhi highlighted Kanshi Ram's significant role in social justice and empowering marginalized communities. This call follows consistent demands from Dalit leaders and intellectuals for the highest national honor for the BSP founder.

title: Iran war, crude prices to steer Dalal Street this week
content: Indian equity markets face a volatile week, driven by Middle East conflict developments and crude oil price movements. Investors are also keenly watching the US Federal Reserve's interest rate decision and inflation data. Steep losses were recorded last week amid rising oil, a weakening rupee, and foreign fund outflows.

title: ‘Terrible rat race for medical, engineering seats’: What Madras high court said on Indian education
content: The Madras High Court allowed a CBSE student to take mathematics as an additional subject, criticizing India's education system's focus on admissions over learning. The court emphasized correcting procedural errors when factual evidence shows a student studied the subject, highlighting the judiciary's role in balancing rules with student welfare.

title: ‘Child trusted him as a grandfather’: Mumbai court convicts 79-year-old man in Pocso case
content: A 79-year-old man was sentenced to three years of simple imprisonment for sexually assaulting a 10-year-old girl. The court found the accused guilty of luring the child into his home and kissing her, deeming her testimony reliable despite defense claims. The judge considered the accused's advanced age when awarding the sentence.

title: Honour killing: Couple murdered by woman’s father, cousin in Gujarat
content: A young couple's disappearance in Gujarat has been revealed as a brutal honor killing. The 19-year-old woman's father allegedly orchestrated their murders because he opposed their marriage. Police recovered the decomposed bodies from a well, leading to the arrest of the father and his nephew.

title: ‘Good stocks in hand’: India well placed on crude, fuel despite Middle East war, says Goyal
content: India is well-prepared for Middle East crisis disruptions, with ample fuel stocks and no supply issues reported.  The government is diversifying gas import sources and will soon announce support for affected exporters, including insurance schemes.  Despite short-term economic pressures and rupee stress, India remains confident in its growth trajectory.

title: Who was Madhu Malhotra? 'Satte Pe Satta' and 'Hero' actress who passed away at age of 71
content: Beloved star Madhu Malhotra, recognized for her captivating performances in classics like 'Satte Pe Satta' and 'Hero', sadly departed at the age of 71 on March 13. With a prolific career that flourished during the 80s and 90s, she graced over 100 films, leaving a lasting impact with standout roles in both 'The Great Gambler' and 'Satte Pe Satta'. Read on to know more in detail.

title: Pakistan says it hit military facilities inside Afghanistan​, attacks tunnel used by Taliban
content: Pakistan launched strikes on Afghan military facilities and alleged terrorist hideouts in Kandahar, escalating tensions with the Taliban.  These actions follow Pakistan's interception of drone attacks originating from Afghanistan.  The Taliban administration reported limited civilian damage, while Pakistan warned of retaliation after its president stated a "red line" was crossed by targeting civilians.

title: 'I have no interest': BMC removes red-blue beacon from Mumbai mayor Ritu Tawde's car
content: Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde's official vehicle and escort car had their red and blue flashing lights removed following a social media controversy and an RTI activist's objection. The BMC confirmed similar lights on the deputy mayor's and leader of the house's vehicles were also taken down, citing an administrative lapse.

title: 'Phone pakad, social media hata': Abhishek Sharma reveals tough advice from Surya, Gambhir, Pandya
content: Abhishek Sharma faced a challenging start to the T20 World Cup 2026, scoring three consecutive ducks. Supported by captain Suryakumar Yadav and coach Gautam Gambhir, who advised him to disconnect from social media, Sharma regained his form. He played a crucial role in India's title win with a blistering 52 in the final.

title: 'Assaulted and abused her over caste': Bengaluru man kills live-in partner
content: A 23-year-old woman was allegedly murdered by her live-in partner in Bengaluru, who then attempted to stage it as a suicide. Police investigation revealed strangulation, leading to the accused's arrest. The victim had previously complained of frequent assaults and caste-based abuse by her partner. A case has been registered under murder and SC/ST Act provisions.

title: Middle East conflict forces Indian exporters to reroute shipments, absorb higher costs
content: Indian exporters are navigating Middle East conflict fallout, facing rising freight, insurance, and transit times. Businesses are adapting by adjusting inventory and contracts, while shipping lines reroute vessels around Africa, adding significant delays and costs. The government is exploring support measures, including airlifting perishables and alternative port options.

title: Opposition questions EC’s neutrality ahead of state assembly schedule announcement
content: The Election Commission is set to announce the schedule for assembly elections in Assam, Kerala, Puducherry, Tamil Nadu, and West Bengal today at 4 pm. Ahead of the announcement, opposition leaders have voiced concerns, questioning the poll body's neutrality and the effectiveness of the Model Code of Conduct.

title: 'We wanted more firepower': Gautam Gambhir explains bold Sanju Samson move in T20 World Cup
content: India's head coach Gautam Gambhir revealed Sanju Samson's T20 World Cup return was driven by an aggressive batting philosophy, not match-ups. The team aimed for more explosive powerplay starts, emphasizing Samson's game-changing ability. Gambhir also backed opener Abhishek Sharma, urging him to remain fearless and committed to his attacking approach.

title: Middle East War Day 16: UAE and other Gulf countries report fresh attacks; Kuwait airport hit
content: Explosions rocked Dubai as Iran claimed missile and drone strikes on the UAE's Al Dhafra airbase. Kuwait International Airport also reported drone impacts damaging radar, though no injuries occurred.  These actions follow Iran's warnings and accusations of UAE ports being used for attacks on its oil facilities, escalating regional tensions.

title: 'Will shoot you in head': Rapper Badshah gets death threat from Lawrence Bishnoi gang
content: Rapper Badshah has reportedly received a death threat from individuals linked to the Lawrence Bishnoi gang, warning him over his controversial song 'Tateeree'. The threat references a previous nightclub incident and comes after the Haryana State Commission for Women ordered his arrest for failing to appear before them regarding the song's objectionable lyrics.

title: No Messi vs. Yamal: Qatar Finalissima canceled
content: the 2026 edition of the finalissima between Spain and Argentina was cancelled due to the current political situation in the region . the match would have pitted Lamine Yamal against Lionel Messi for the first time . UEFA, CONMEBOL and the local organizing committee have explored alternative dates and venues .

title: Why Patriots QB Drake Maye was a good selling point to free agents
content: new: byard summed it up after spending the last two seasons with the Chicago Bears . new: "an ascending quarterback; it's one of the hardest things to get right in the NFL," Byard says . "i'm excited to be able to play with him," byard says after signing his one-year contract .

title: Premier League live updates: Man United take on Aston Villa
content: This weekend of  Premier League  matches continues with two big games. First, it's a clash between two sides looking to qualify for next season's  UEFA Champions League  as  Manchester United  host  Aston Villa  at Old Trafford. At the same time, Crystal Palace host Leeds United, and Fulham travel to Nottingham Forest. Later at Anfield,  Liverpool  will also look to continue their quest to qualify for next season's Champions League, while their opponents,  Tottenham Hotspur , will be hoping to get away from the relegation zone as quickly as possible. Enjoy all the updates from Sunday's matches.
